title: The Tell-All Drops
briefing: Your former body man has written a memoir, and the leaked excerpts are brutal in the pettiest ways: you eat cereal for dinner, you practice smiling in the elevator, and you once called a senator 'a haircut in a suit.' Publication day is Friday and every outlet has galleys. Do you defuse it, crush it, or outsell it?

choices.label: Laugh it off
choices.detail: Post the midnight cereal photo yourself
choices.result: Your midnight-cereal selfie outperforms the book's first chapter. Historians wince; the internet crowns you.

news.headline: PREZ EATS CEREAL FOR DINNER AND WE LOVE IT 🥣
White House Deflects Memoir With Humor
Levity Is Not a Governing Strategy
At Least Someone in Power Is Relatable

social.text: president posting cereal pics at 1am is the most relatable thing the government has done in years
the office deserves better than a spoon and a smirk

choices.advisor: Cheap laugh, cheap win — just remember the book has eleven more chapters.

choices.label: Send the lawyers
choices.detail: Cease-and-desist before publication day
choices.result: The injunction lands and leaks inside the building dry up overnight. So does the goodwill of every reporter with a byline.

news.headline: White House Moves to Block Former Aide's Memoir
Discipline Returns to the West Wing
Presidents Don't Get to Ban Books
BANNED! THE BOOK THEY DON'T WANT YOU TO READ 📕🚫

social.text: a cease-and-desist is just a bestseller sticker with extra steps

choices.advisor: You've bought silence at retail price. Pray the appeal is quiet too.

echo.title: Streisand in Chief
echo.text: The injunction fails and the memoir debuts at number one, with your lawsuit as its marketing campaign.

choices.label: Announce your own memoir
choices.detail: Sign a rival book deal on the spot
choices.result: Publishers bid eight figures before lunch. Your staff spends the afternoon wondering which chapter they'll be in.

news.headline: President's Book Deal Shows Market Confidence
Dueling Memoirs Set Up Publishing Showdown
BOOK WARS! PREZ VS. BODY MAN 📚⚔️

social.text: the aide's book: gossip. the president's book: gossip with an approval rating
everyone in this building is suddenly very nice to me

choices.advisor: Nothing focuses a leaker's mind like knowing you write last.

title: The Doctor's Quiet Word
briefing: Your annual physical turned up an irregular heartbeat — minor, fixable, and politically radioactive. The physician can do the procedure quietly at Camp David under 'scheduled downtime,' or you can tell the country before the rumor mill does. Markets twitch at the word 'cardiac.' Disclose, or disappear for a weekend?

choices.label: Address the nation
choices.detail: Full medical briefing, cameras on
choices.result: You narrate your own EKG on live television. Markets dip, late night writes itself, and something like trust creeps upward.

news.headline: President Discloses Heart Procedure, Prognosis Excellent
Markets Wobble on Health News
Radical Transparency, For Once
PREZ HEART SCARE — SAYS 'I'M FINE, RELAX' 💓

social.text: an irregular heartbeat and a completely regular press conference. refreshing.

choices.advisor: You traded one bad news cycle for a credibility deposit. Decent rate.

choices.label: Schedule a quiet weekend
choices.detail: Camp David, no cameras, no announcement
choices.result: The procedure takes forty minutes; the cover story takes eleven staffers. Everyone who knows is now someone who could talk.

news.headline: President Spends Restful Weekend at Camp David
Steady Hand, Steady Markets
WHERE'S THE PREZ?? WEEKEND VANISHING ACT 👀

social.text: two canceled rallies and a 'personal weekend.' I have questions

choices.advisor: Secrets in this town have a shelf life. Hope the heart outlasts the story.

echo.title: The Medical Records Leak
echo.text: A billing clerk's screenshot surfaces: the 'restful weekend' was a cardiac procedure. The condition was minor; the cover-up isn't.

title: First Dog Goes Corporate
briefing: Biscuit, the First Dog, has forty million followers, a fan-run merch black market, and now a formal licensing offer: plush toys, a breakfast cereal, an animated series. Ethics counsel is chewing antacids while children's hospitals quietly note what the proceeds could fund. Monetize the mutt, or keep the paws clean?

choices.label: License it all for charity
choices.detail: Every royalty goes to children's hospitals
choices.result: Biscuit plushies sell out in four hours and a pediatric wing gets a naming gift. Ethics counsel files a memo titled 'Unprecedented, Concerning, Adorable.'

news.headline: BISCUIT MANIA!! FIRST DOG SAVES SICK KIDS 🐕💖
First Dog Licensing Deal to Fund Hospitals
Cute, But Who Audits the Dog?
The Free Market Loves a Good Boy

social.text: I bought four Biscuits. for the children. obviously

choices.advisor: Nobody's ever lost votes to a golden retriever. Just keep the paperwork spotless.

choices.label: Keep the paws clean
choices.detail: No merchandising, no exceptions
choices.result: The ethics office frames your refusal memo. Forty million followers post crying emojis, and a bootleg plush industry thrives without you.

news.headline: White House Declines First Dog Licensing Offer
A Rare 'No' to Merchandising Everything
NO BISCUIT TOYS?! INTERNET IN MOURNING 😭

social.text: somewhere an ethics lawyer just felt joy for the first time in years
we would have paid ANYTHING

choices.advisor: Clean hands, empty hospital wing. That's the trade — own it.

title: The State Dinner Collapses
briefing: Mid-toast at the state dinner, the visiting Prime Minister's translator faints into the consommé, the soufflé course catches fire, and a pop-star guest livestreams the whole thing to nine million people. The Prime Minister looks like he wants the floor to open. The room is waiting for you to speak. Grace, or comedy?

choices.label: Grab the mic and roll
choices.detail: Toast 'the most memorable dinner in White House history'
choices.result: The room roars, the clip goes global, and the Prime Minister manages a thin smile his delegation later describes as 'diplomatic.'

news.headline: FLAMING SOUFFLÉ, ICE-COLD PREZ 🔥😎
State Dinner Mishaps Met With Improvised Toast
Allies Deserve Dignity, Not a Comedy Set
Shout Out the Kitchen Staff Who Actually Saved Dinner

social.text: best. dinner. ever. 🔥
somewhere a chief of protocol is drafting a resignation letter

choices.advisor: You won the room and iced the guest. Hope his memory is short.

echo.title: The Prime Minister's Toast
echo.text: At his own state banquet, the Prime Minister retells the flaming-soufflé story as 'the night our friendship was forged' — and toasts you by name.

choices.label: End the evening with grace
choices.detail: Cut the program, check on the translator, apologize privately
choices.result: The translator recovers and the Prime Minister's office issues warm thanks. At home, the clip of you soberly ending the party gets recut as 'the fun police.'

news.headline: President Cuts Dinner Short After Medical Incident
Composed, Traditional, Presidential
PARTY'S OVER — PREZ PULLS PLUG ON CHAOS NIGHT 🚨

social.text: he really said 'the evening is concluded' like a substitute teacher

choices.advisor: Boring is underrated. The alliance will remember; the internet won't.

title: An Old Friend Calls
briefing: Your college roommate — best man at your wedding, keeper of your worst stories — calls the private line. His daughter wants a White House fellowship 'fast-tracked,' and he'd love 'five minutes' with the Commerce Secretary about his startup. He knew you before the motorcade, and he's never asked for anything. Until now.

choices.label: Make the quiet calls
choices.detail: Fast-track the fellowship, arrange the meeting
choices.result: The daughter starts Monday; the Secretary takes the meeting. Your friend texts a photo from your dorm days captioned 'still the best.'

news.headline: New Fellowship Class Announced at White House
WHO'S THE NEW INTERN? SOURCES SAY 'CONNECTED' 👀
Merit Is for People Without Roommates

social.text: applied three times. cool cool cool.

choices.advisor: Loyalty is a currency, but the exchange rate to scandal is brutal.

echo.title: The Fellowship Files
echo.text: A transparency group publishes the fast-track email chain. The favor was small; the headline — 'RULES FOR THEE' — is not.

choices.label: Tell him to apply like everyone
choices.detail: No calls, no meetings, one long apology over beers
choices.result: He goes quiet for a week, then sends a two-word text: 'Get it.' The fellowship office never knows how close it came.

news.headline: White House Fellowship Selections Proceed on Schedule
Imagine: A Process That's Just a Process
Friendship Tested, Standards Kept

social.text: the scandals you never hear about are the system working

choices.advisor: You just paid full price for integrity. It compounds — slowly.

echo.title: The Best Man's Toast
echo.text: At an alumni gala, your roommate tells the story of the favor you refused — 'and that,' he says, 'is why I trust him with the country.' The clip travels.

title: Roast Night at the Correspondents
briefing: The correspondents' dinner is Saturday and your writers have delivered two speeches: a flame-thrower set torching every outlet that's torched you, and a self-deprecating act that ends with you toasting the free press. The Beacon's editor is seated at table one, arms already crossed. Which speech goes in the teleprompter?

choices.label: Run the flame-thrower set
choices.detail: Every outlet gets a verse
choices.result: The ballroom gasps, your base makes highlight reels, and four bureau chiefs leave before dessert. The jokes were fair; the invoices arrive Monday.

news.headline: PREZ TORCHES PRESS TO THEIR FACES 🔥🎤
Finally, Someone Says It
Punching Down From the Podium
President's Roast Draws Laughs, and Walkouts

social.text: the 'thoughts and prayers for the editorial board' line?? unhinged. iconic.

choices.advisor: Great set. Now every correction you ever need will run above the fold.

echo.title: Frost in the Briefing Room
echo.text: Your next stumble gets wall-to-wall coverage and zero benefit of the doubt. The press corps' memory, it turns out, is professional-grade.

choices.label: Run the humble set
choices.detail: You're the punchline; the press gets the toast
choices.result: You bomb two jokes on purpose and land the toast. The room warms; your base's group chats call it 'surrender with a salad fork.'

news.headline: President Pokes Fun — Mostly at Himself
A Commander-in-Chief Should Not Grovel for Laughs
Self-Awareness Sighted in Washington

social.text: we wanted a fighter, we got a toastmaster

choices.advisor: You banked goodwill with people who buy ink by the barrel. Cheap insurance.

choices.label: Skip the dinner entirely
choices.detail: Visit a night-shift firehouse instead
choices.result: The firehouse photos are gold; the empty head-table chair becomes the night's longest-running joke, told entirely at your expense.

news.headline: PREZ GHOSTS THE PRESS — HANGS WITH FIREFIGHTERS 🚒
President Skips Correspondents' Dinner, First in Decades
Dodging Scrutiny Isn't a Photo Op

social.text: he did dishes. actual dishes.

choices.advisor: You can dodge the roast, not the reckoning. They'll write about that empty chair for weeks.

title: Hot Mic, Cold Sweat
briefing: After the summit photo-op, you leaned to an aide and said the allied Chancellor 'negotiates like a toddler with a treaty.' The mic was live. Eighty million views, a diplomatic 'request for clarification,' and a meme format that will outlive us all. The Chancellor's plane is still on your tarmac.

choices.label: Apologize before the plane leaves
choices.detail: Personal call, then a public statement
choices.result: The Chancellor accepts with a frosty 'we've all been tired.' At home, the meme accounts declare you no fun; abroad, the alliance exhales.

news.headline: President Apologizes for Hot-Mic Remark
Apologizing Abroad for Telling the Truth
Repairing Damage Is Not Weakness
SORRY NOT SORRY? PREZ SAYS SORRY 😬

social.text: RIP toddler treaty format, killed by diplomacy

choices.advisor: Eat the joke, keep the ally. That's the whole job some days.

choices.label: Own it
choices.detail: 'I said what everyone in that room was thinking'
choices.result: Your base prints t-shirts by dinnertime. The Chancellor's office cancels a joint appearance and refers all questions to 'the toddler desk.'

news.headline: 'I SAID WHAT I SAID' — PREZ GOES FULL SAVAGE 💅
Strength Abroad Starts With Candor
Allied Relations Strained After President Declines to Apologize
Alliances Are Not Content

social.text: TODDLER WITH A TREATY merch live in 2 hours

choices.advisor: The shirts are funny. The trade summit next quarter will not be.

echo.title: The Toddler Tax
echo.text: At trade talks, the Chancellor's delegation slow-walks every clause. Officials deny it's personal, in a tone that confirms it's personal.

choices.label: Invite the Chancellor to Camp David
choices.detail: No apology — just eighteen holes and a long dinner
choices.result: The Chancellor arrives stone-faced and leaves laughing at a joke nobody will repeat. Neither of you says the word 'toddler.' The photos do the diplomacy.

news.headline: Leaders Meet at Camp David After Hot-Mic Flap
GOLF DIPLOMACY!! WHO WON? (WE MAY NEVER KNOW) ⛳
Must Be Nice to Golf Your Way Out of Apologies

social.text: the handshake at the 18th looked... genuinely warm? plot twist

choices.advisor: An apology says sorry once. A tee time says it for four hours.

title: Paint Me Like History
briefing: The Portrait Commission needs your answer by Friday: a Grand Manner oil — columns, ceremonial desk, gravitas by the gallon — or a piece by a controversial young painter whose work critics call 'genius or vandalism, possibly both.' Your presidential library architect is holding on line two with matching questions. What image gets to outlive you?

choices.label: Commission the Grand Manner oil
choices.detail: Columns, flag, the classic gaze into history
choices.result: The sitting takes six weeks and the result is undeniably presidential — reviewers use words like 'safe,' 'stately,' and 'screensaver.'

news.headline: A Portrait Worthy of the Office
Presidential Portrait to Follow Traditional Style
SNOOZE ART! PREZ PICKS BEIGE IMMORTALITY 🖼️😴

social.text: it's fine. it's a portrait. history will scroll past it

choices.advisor: Nobody ever lost the future by boring it. Probably.

choices.label: Hire the controversial modernist
choices.detail: Neon undertones, fractured perspective, no desk
choices.result: The unveiling divides the art world in the best possible way. Half the country sees a masterpiece; the other half sees a president mid-glitch.

news.headline: Finally, a President Who Trusts Artists
IS HE MELTING?? PORTRAIT BREAKS THE INTERNET 🫠🎨
The Office Deserves a Desk, Not a Fever Dream
Modernist Presidential Portrait Sparks National Debate

social.text: museums will fight over this in 50 years. screenshot me

choices.advisor: You just picked a fight with taste itself. Bold. Genuinely can't call it.

choices.label: Put it to a public vote
choices.detail: Three finalists, one national poll
choices.result: Democracy chooses a portrait of you giving a thumbs-up next to Biscuit. Turnout is spectacular. Art historians request asylum.

news.headline: AMERICA VOTES: THUMBS-UP DOG PORTRAIT WINS 🐕👍
Public Selects Presidential Portrait in Online Vote
Art by Poll Is How We Got This Timeline

social.text: I voted for the dog one and I'd do it again

choices.advisor: You gave the people what they wanted. History's a tougher audience.

title: The West Wing Runs Empty
briefing: Your deputy chief of staff fell asleep in the Situation Room — during a briefing he was giving. Three schedulers quit this week, and the speechwriting shop is communicating entirely in typos. The Chief of Staff proposes a mandatory rest rotation right in the middle of your biggest legislative push. Power through, or power down?

choices.label: Order the rest rotation
choices.detail: Mandatory leave, staggered coverage, no exceptions
choices.result: For two weeks the building runs at half-speed and nothing catches fire. The bill slips a month; the deputy chief sends a postcard that just says 'THANK YOU.'

news.headline: White House Staff Placed on Rest Rotation
Imagine: A Boss Who Lets Workers Sleep
The Agenda Doesn't Nap

social.text: a government office admitting humans need rest? unprecedented

choices.advisor: We lose a month now or lose the whole senior staff by spring. Easy math, hard optics.

choices.label: Push through the window
choices.detail: Espresso bar, hazard bonuses, victory or collapse
choices.result: The bill clears its hurdle at 4 a.m. to bleary applause. In the hallway, a scheduler updates her résumé with the desk lamp off, so no one sees.

news.headline: Relentless West Wing Delivers
Legislative Win Comes Amid Staff Strain Reports
The Burnout Bill: Passed on Broken Backs
4AM VOTE!! WEST WING RUNNING ON FUMES AND ESPRESSO ☕😵

social.text: 'hazard bonus' for a desk job should tell you everything

choices.advisor: We got the win. Now watch who doesn't show up Monday.

echo.title: The Resignation Wave
echo.text: Five senior aides resign in one news cycle, each with a politely devastating letter. The rebuild costs you a quarter of momentum.

title: The Empty Calendar Gambit
briefing: It's the quietest news week of your term, and the First Family has a beach house on hold and a teenager threatening 'memoirs of a neglected childhood' if you cancel again. Aides warn that presidents on paddleboards become metaphors within the hour. The alternative: stay, and squeeze a policy blitz out of the silence.

choices.label: Take the real vacation
choices.detail: One week, no press pool on the beach
choices.result: You fall off the paddleboard exactly once, and the photo is everywhere within minutes. Your kid teaches you a card game; you can't remember the last time you laughed like that.

news.headline: SPLASH-IN-CHIEF!! PREZ WIPES OUT 🌊🏄
Who's Minding the Store?
President Begins Week-Long Family Vacation
Rest Is Labor Policy Too

social.text: respectfully, that wipeout form was terrible. welcome to the club

choices.advisor: The wipeout costs a point. The recharge might save the term. I'll take that trade.

choices.label: Take a working vacation
choices.detail: The beach house becomes the 'Coastal White House'
choices.result: You sign two orders in flip-flops and take a briefing under an umbrella. The family gets you at half-strength; the country gets a tan president. Nobody is fully satisfied.

news.headline: President Balances Vacation With Official Duties
Working Vacation Keeps Markets Calm
BRIEFINGS IN FLIP-FLOPS!! COASTAL WHITE HOUSE VIBES 🩴

social.text: the motorcade has beach towels now. this is my life

choices.advisor: Half a vacation rests half a president. Better than none, worse than either.

choices.label: Cancel and blitz
choices.detail: Surprise policy week while rivals sleep
choices.result: You dominate a dead news week and clear the backlog. The beach house deposit is gone, and dinner that Sunday is very, very quiet.

news.headline: White House Launches Surprise Policy Week
No Days Off: Executive Tempo Impresses
Even Presidents Need a Union Rep
VACATION CANCELED!! WORKAHOLIC-IN-CHIEF STRIKES AGAIN 😤

social.text: he really saw an empty calendar and said 'mine'

choices.advisor: You bought a great week. Check what it cost at the residence.

echo.title: Running on Empty
echo.text: Mid-press conference, you blank on your own signature initiative for nine seconds. The clip loops all weekend; the word 'exhausted' trends.
